regulation_links.obligation_summary: Access controls, audit controls, and transmission security.
Special category data protections and lawful processing constraints.
Large-scale export of special-category health data requires strict lawful basis and safeguards.
Transmission security and integrity protections for health data messages.
Integrity controls required to protect against improper alteration of ePHI.
Data minimization and confidentiality principles apply to imaging metadata handling.
Cyber risk management and incident handling for essential entities.
Device cybersecurity lifecycle and vulnerability management obligations.
Appropriate technical and organizational measures for secure processing and access control.
Controlled-substance prescribing and tracking safeguards.
High-risk AI risk management and data governance controls.
Person or entity authentication requirements for access to ePHI.
Secondary-use health data governance and access constraints.
Imaging metadata and payload access must meet minimization and confidentiality principles.
Networked device command/control safeguards and secure lifecycle evidence are expected for regulated devices.
Controlled-substance refill workflows require anti-fraud controls and auditable prescribing integrity.
Transmission integrity and security controls apply to covered electronic healthcare transaction pathways.
Appropriate technical and organizational measures are required for secure remote-health telemetry processing.
Cross-enterprise document-sharing infrastructure must implement robust security and integrity safeguards.
Identity accuracy and integrity are essential to lawful, fair, and accurate processing of health data.
Integrity controls are required to prevent improper alteration and destruction of ePHI.
Person or entity authentication controls are required for trusted client onboarding and access.
Transmission security and integrity controls apply to inbound clinical-document exchange channels.
Integrity controls must protect against improper alteration of coded clinical content and associated ePHI semantics.
Accuracy, integrity, and confidentiality principles require robust controls for terminology and coding correctness.
Supply-chain cybersecurity risk management expectations.

control_links.control_summary: Least privilege for API and app-level access.
Information flow enforcement for API data export boundaries.
Transmission confidentiality and integrity protection.
Comprehensive audit generation for record/order modifications.
Data-at-rest/in-transit protections and minimization.
Asset and software lifecycle integrity management.
Software and firmware integrity protections.
Strong identity and authentication control mechanisms.
Separation of duties in high-risk medication workflows.
Development process and model/data integrity assurance.
Authenticator management and anti-replay protections.
Continuous monitoring for anomalous access and exfiltration patterns.
Constrain information flow and export pathways for imaging APIs.
Protect command and software integrity for network-connected clinical devices.
Strong authentication and verification in prescribing/refill workflows.
Generate auditable evidence for transaction transformations and integrity decisions.
Detect anomalous remote telemetry identity and behavior patterns.
Comprehensive auditing for cross-community registry/document metadata changes.
Least-privilege and verification controls for identity-correlation and record retrieval operations.
Controlled and approved configuration/model change management for clinical templates.
Credential lifecycle governance for trusted client registration and certificate material.
Input/document validation controls to prevent malformed or malicious payload ingestion.
Formal configuration and change-control enforcement for terminology-value-set and concept-map updates.
Supply-chain risk response and third-party assurance controls.
